item.title: SP-FSOD: Semantic-Visual Purification Guided Few-shot Object Detection on Remote Sensing Images
item.dc:creator: Jingzheng Tu
Yan Yan
Ying Lu
Cailian Chen
Xinping Guan
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Few-shot object detection (FSOD) on remote sensing images is a promising technology for remote sensing applications, as it enhances generalization capability. Most works are devoted to excavating discriminative and compact visual feature representations for FSOD, without considering other domain knowledge. With the rapid development of large language models, promoting FSOD performance with the help of large language models becomes possible. However, the domain gap between text and image is unignorable. In this paper, we propose a semantic-visual purification guided FSOD method for remote sensing images (SP-FSOD) to improve performance. Firstly, a feature purification memory is designed to store old knowledge. Secondly, a semanticvisual triplet loss is proposed to alleviate the domain discrepancy between text and image. Thirdly, a flexible fusion module is presented to fuse the feature maps of the semantic and visual domains adaptively. Experiments on large-scale public datasets demonstrate that the proposed SP-FSOD method achieves the state-of-the-art performance on three-shot, five-shot, and ten-shot settings. Published: 2026-06-12T19:42:30+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.816 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jingzheng Tu; Yan Yan; Ying Lu; Cailian Chen; Xinping Guan

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3703121

Relevance: 0.816 (must_read)

Few-shot object detection (FSOD) on remote sensing images is a promising technology for remote sensing applications, as it enhances generalization capability. Most works are devoted to excavating discriminative and compact visual feature representations for FSOD, without considering other domain knowledge. With the rapid development of large language models, promoting FSOD performance with the help of large language models becomes possible. However, the domain gap between text and image is unignorable. In this paper, we propose a semantic-visual purification guided FSOD method for remote sensing images (SP-FSOD) to improve performance. Firstly, a feature purification memory is designed to store old knowledge. Secondly, a semanticvisual triplet loss is proposed to alleviate the domain discrepancy between text and image. Thirdly, a flexible fusion module is presented to fuse the feature maps of the semantic and visual domains adaptively. Experiments on large-scale public datasets demonstrate that the proposed SP-FSOD method achieves the state-of-the-art performance on three-shot, five-shot, and ten-shot settings.

item.title: Hyperbolic Prototype Guidance for Incremental SAR ATR
item.dc:creator: Yanjie Xu
Hao Sun
Chenfang Liu
Linbin Zhang
Kefeng Ji
Gangyao Kuang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Despite the success of deep learning methods in synthetic aperture radar automatic target recognition (SAR ATR), they face significant challenges in open environments. Deep models suffer from catastrophic forgetting when learning new classes. Moreover, SAR images are frequently degraded by serious noise, sidelobes, and clutter, necessitating robust representations. Existing methods typically employ Euclidean space optimization, which is not suitable for modeling SAR targets with hierarchical semantics. In contrast, hyperbolic space naturally captures hierarchical structures, demonstrating advantages in mitigating catastrophic forgetting and providing robust representation. In this article, we propose Hyperbolic Prototype Guidance (HPG) framework for incremental SAR ATR. First, HPG constructs a hierarchical topology in the adaptive hyperbolic embedding space. Specifically, the deep features of SAR images are projected onto a Poincaré ball and utilized to generate multi-level prototypes, thereby constructing a hierarchical topology. To accommodate the dynamic data distribution, complexity-aware curvature modulation provides a specific hyperbolic space tailored to the current topology. Secondly, HPG utilizes local sensitive prototype regularization to learn new classes, where the hierarchical topology undergoes local structural expansion to align with evolving SAR target taxonomies. Finally, HPG utilizes hierarchical prototype relation distillation to preserve the representation of old classes, where the global topological construction is maintained during incremental learning, alleviating catastrophic forgetting. Extensive experiments on both class-incremental and data incremental learning across three benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of HPG, achieving state-of-the-art performance in incremental SAR ATR. Published: 2026-06-12T19:42:49+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.813 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yanjie Xu; Hao Sun; Chenfang Liu; Linbin Zhang; Kefeng Ji; Gangyao Kuang

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3703185

Relevance: 0.813 (must_read)

Despite the success of deep learning methods in synthetic aperture radar automatic target recognition (SAR ATR), they face significant challenges in open environments. Deep models suffer from catastrophic forgetting when learning new classes. Moreover, SAR images are frequently degraded by serious noise, sidelobes, and clutter, necessitating robust representations. Existing methods typically employ Euclidean space optimization, which is not suitable for modeling SAR targets with hierarchical semantics. In contrast, hyperbolic space naturally captures hierarchical structures, demonstrating advantages in mitigating catastrophic forgetting and providing robust representation. In this article, we propose Hyperbolic Prototype Guidance (HPG) framework for incremental SAR ATR. First, HPG constructs a hierarchical topology in the adaptive hyperbolic embedding space. Specifically, the deep features of SAR images are projected onto a Poincaré ball and utilized to generate multi-level prototypes, thereby constructing a hierarchical topology. To accommodate the dynamic data distribution, complexity-aware curvature modulation provides a specific hyperbolic space tailored to the current topology. Secondly, HPG utilizes local sensitive prototype regularization to learn new classes, where the hierarchical topology undergoes local structural expansion to align with evolving SAR target taxonomies. Finally, HPG utilizes hierarchical prototype relation distillation to preserve the representation of old classes, where the global topological construction is maintained during incremental learning, alleviating catastrophic forgetting. Extensive experiments on both class-incremental and data incremental learning across three benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of HPG, achieving state-of-the-art performance in incremental SAR ATR.

item.title: Correlation-Guided Progressive Interaction and Gated Fusion for Multi-Modal Optical and SAR Remote Sensing Image Object Detection
item.dc:creator: Xiao-Nan Jiang
Ze-Xian Huang
Fan-Lu Wu
Yao Fu
Shi-Jie Fan
Xin Gao
Ao Han
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Multi-modal remote sensing object detection faces challenges of significant nonlinear imaging discrepancies and semantic feature misalignment between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery. Existing fusion algorithms often struggle to effectively distinguish complementary information from modality-specific noise, leading to feature redundancy or even negative transfer. To address these issues, this paper proposes a feature-level fusion strategy based on correlation-guided progressive interaction and gated fusion, which is embedded into a single-stage detection network to enhance multi-modal detection performance. For the feature enhancement stage, a Correlation-Guided Progressive Spatial Interaction (CGPSI) module is designed. This module utilizes Cross-Modal Spatial Correlation Modeling (CMSCM) to construct guidance priors and couples them with a Progressive Spatial Modulation (PSM) mechanism to generate spatial weight maps that dynamically evolve with the training process. Simultaneously, bidirectional parallel Attention-based Cross-Modal Retrieval (ACMR) is employed to capture complementary contextual features across a global receptive field. Subsequently, the generated dynamic weights are utilized to perform fine-grained spatial weighting and filtering, accurately extracting discriminative gains and suppressing cross-modal noise interference. Finally, the modulated complementary information is integrated into the backbone path through residual connections to achieve the deep integration of complementary information, significantly strengthening the representation capability of target features. For the feature fusion stage, a Gated Weight Fusion (GWF) module is adopted to identify discriminative differences in dual-modal features, effectively suppressing background noise and clutter interference in complex scenes by generating adaptive spatial weight maps. Experimental results on the OGSOD (Optical Image Guided SAR Image Object Detection) dataset demon... Published: 2026-06-12T19:42:49+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.811 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiao-Nan Jiang; Ze-Xian Huang; Fan-Lu Wu; Yao Fu; Shi-Jie Fan; Xin Gao; Ao Han

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3703125

Relevance: 0.811 (must_read)

Multi-modal remote sensing object detection faces challenges of significant nonlinear imaging discrepancies and semantic feature misalignment between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery. Existing fusion algorithms often struggle to effectively distinguish complementary information from modality-specific noise, leading to feature redundancy or even negative transfer. To address these issues, this paper proposes a feature-level fusion strategy based on correlation-guided progressive interaction and gated fusion, which is embedded into a single-stage detection network to enhance multi-modal detection performance. For the feature enhancement stage, a Correlation-Guided Progressive Spatial Interaction (CGPSI) module is designed. This module utilizes Cross-Modal Spatial Correlation Modeling (CMSCM) to construct guidance priors and couples them with a Progressive Spatial Modulation (PSM) mechanism to generate spatial weight maps that dynamically evolve with the training process. Simultaneously, bidirectional parallel Attention-based Cross-Modal Retrieval (ACMR) is employed to capture complementary contextual features across a global receptive field. Subsequently, the generated dynamic weights are utilized to perform fine-grained spatial weighting and filtering, accurately extracting discriminative gains and suppressing cross-modal noise interference. Finally, the modulated complementary information is integrated into the backbone path through residual connections to achieve the deep integration of complementary information, significantly strengthening the representation capability of target features. For the feature fusion stage, a Gated Weight Fusion (GWF) module is adopted to identify discriminative differences in dual-modal features, effectively suppressing background noise and clutter interference in complex scenes by generating adaptive spatial weight maps. Experimental results on the OGSOD (Optical Image Guided SAR Image Object Detection) dataset demon...

item.title: Bistatic SAR Target Recognition Using Transferred Monostatic Knowledge and Cross-Domain Feature Fusion
item.dc:creator: Mingqing Han
Bing Sun
Zhirong Men
Yiheng Zhou
Yihang Zhi
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Bistatic synthetic aperture radar (SAR) systems offer advantages for target recognition but suffer from severe data scarcity, particularly for maritime targets, which hinders the development of data-driven deep learning methods. To address this, this paper constructs a comprehensive electromagnetic simulation dataset containing both monostatic and bistatic SAR images for three ship classes. Leveraging this dataset, we propose a novel target recognition framework that integrates transfer learning with a cross-domain dynamic feature fusion strategy. The core innovation is a hybrid attention-based feature fusion network, combined with domain-adversarial training, designed to effectively transfer and fuse knowledge from abundant monostatic SAR data to assist recognition under limited bistatic samples. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, which achieves an F1-score of 68.08%, outperforming baseline approaches and confirming its capability to enhance bistatic SAR target recognition with scarce annotated data. Published: 2026-06-12T19:42:49+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.810 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mingqing Han; Bing Sun; Zhirong Men; Yiheng Zhou; Yihang Zhi

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3703173

Relevance: 0.810 (must_read)

Bistatic synthetic aperture radar (SAR) systems offer advantages for target recognition but suffer from severe data scarcity, particularly for maritime targets, which hinders the development of data-driven deep learning methods. To address this, this paper constructs a comprehensive electromagnetic simulation dataset containing both monostatic and bistatic SAR images for three ship classes. Leveraging this dataset, we propose a novel target recognition framework that integrates transfer learning with a cross-domain dynamic feature fusion strategy. The core innovation is a hybrid attention-based feature fusion network, combined with domain-adversarial training, designed to effectively transfer and fuse knowledge from abundant monostatic SAR data to assist recognition under limited bistatic samples. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, which achieves an F1-score of 68.08%, outperforming baseline approaches and confirming its capability to enhance bistatic SAR target recognition with scarce annotated data.

item.title: Domain Adaptive Remote Sensing Scene Recognition via Transferable Discriminative Visual Prompting
item.dc:creator: Zihao Fu
Lianxu Yang
Shengsheng Wang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: The task of remote sensing scene recognition suffers from significant distribution shifts induced by complex and variable observation environments, making unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) a promising solution. However, existing prompt-based UDA methods mainly focus on token-level alignment within either modality and often overlook structural adaptability and spatially discriminative regions. In this paper, we propose a novel framework termed Domain Adaptive Remote Sensing Scene Recognition via Transferable Discriminative Visual Prompting (TDVP) to overcome these limitations. Specifically, we introduce the Discriminative Region Guidance Module, an architectural enhancement designed to inject region-specific discriminative representations directly into the vision encoder. Built upon this foundation is a learnable cross-domain semantic prompt, which leverages a region-aware mask to generate spatially adaptive representations that facilitate semantic alignment and enhance transferability across domains. Finally, we introduce prompt consistency regularization, which enforces semantic consistency between visual representations obtained with and without the region-aware mask, thereby enhancing the stability and generalizability of learned prompts across domains. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach effectively addresses domain shifts and consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods. Published: 2026-06-12T19:42:30+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.803 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zihao Fu; Lianxu Yang; Shengsheng Wang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3703098

Relevance: 0.803 (must_read)

The task of remote sensing scene recognition suffers from significant distribution shifts induced by complex and variable observation environments, making unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) a promising solution. However, existing prompt-based UDA methods mainly focus on token-level alignment within either modality and often overlook structural adaptability and spatially discriminative regions. In this paper, we propose a novel framework termed Domain Adaptive Remote Sensing Scene Recognition via Transferable Discriminative Visual Prompting (TDVP) to overcome these limitations. Specifically, we introduce the Discriminative Region Guidance Module, an architectural enhancement designed to inject region-specific discriminative representations directly into the vision encoder. Built upon this foundation is a learnable cross-domain semantic prompt, which leverages a region-aware mask to generate spatially adaptive representations that facilitate semantic alignment and enhance transferability across domains. Finally, we introduce prompt consistency regularization, which enforces semantic consistency between visual representations obtained with and without the region-aware mask, thereby enhancing the stability and generalizability of learned prompts across domains. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach effectively addresses domain shifts and consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods.

item.title: 模态可靠性建模的航空遥感可见光-红外目标检测
item.dc:creator: Yu Dong
Zhang Chunjie
Zhang Xiaoyu
Zheng Xiaolong
item.description: 目的航空遥感可见光与红外（red-green-blue and infrared， RGB-IR）目标检测中，不同模态对检测任务的贡献会随成像条件动态变化。现有方法虽能在一定程度上利用条件信息调节模态融合，但对与模态可靠性直接相关的质量属性的显式建模仍然不足，难以根据成像条件变化自适应调节不同模态对检测任务的贡献。针对上述问题，本文提出一种基于模态可靠性建模的航空遥感RGB-IR目标检测方法，通过语义先验蒸馏引导检测网络学习检测导向的模态可靠性表征，并实现可见光与红外模态的自适应融合。方法首先，构建面向无人机场景的模态质量属性描述数据集，对影响检测性能的关键成像因素进行结构化表达。然后，利用视觉语言模型对属性描述文本进行编码，形成与模态可靠性相关的语义先验，并通过训练阶段的蒸馏监督与属性监督，引导检测网络学习检测导向的模态可靠性表征。最后，从场景级全局可靠性和位置级局部空间可靠性两个层面联合建模可见光与红外模态的有效性，实现面向目标检测的动态自适应融合。结果在DroneVehicle和VEDAI两个公开RGB-IR数据集上，所提方法均取得了较优性能。其中，在DroneVehicle上的mAP@0.5和mAP@0.5：0.95分别达到79.7%和53.7%；在VEDAI上分别达到67.1%和30.1%，并在夜间、弱光及复杂干扰场景下表现出更好的检测精度与鲁棒性。消融实验进一步验证了模态质量属性建模、语义先验蒸馏和全局-局部模态可靠性联合建模的有效性。结论所提方法能够以较低开销将视觉语言模型的模态质量感知能力迁移至检测网络内部，在无需测试阶段额外引入大模型分支的条件下，有效建模复杂成像条件下的模态可靠性变化，提升航空遥感红外与可见光目标检测的精度与鲁棒性。 Published: 2026-06-12T07:54:20+00:00 Venue: Journal of Image and Graphics Score: 0.797 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yu Dong; Zhang Chunjie; Zhang Xiaoyu; Zheng Xiaolong

Journal: Journal of Image and Graphics

DOI: 10.11834/jig.260197

Relevance: 0.797 (must_read)

目的航空遥感可见光与红外（red-green-blue and infrared， RGB-IR）目标检测中，不同模态对检测任务的贡献会随成像条件动态变化。现有方法虽能在一定程度上利用条件信息调节模态融合，但对与模态可靠性直接相关的质量属性的显式建模仍然不足，难以根据成像条件变化自适应调节不同模态对检测任务的贡献。针对上述问题，本文提出一种基于模态可靠性建模的航空遥感RGB-IR目标检测方法，通过语义先验蒸馏引导检测网络学习检测导向的模态可靠性表征，并实现可见光与红外模态的自适应融合。方法首先，构建面向无人机场景的模态质量属性描述数据集，对影响检测性能的关键成像因素进行结构化表达。然后，利用视觉语言模型对属性描述文本进行编码，形成与模态可靠性相关的语义先验，并通过训练阶段的蒸馏监督与属性监督，引导检测网络学习检测导向的模态可靠性表征。最后，从场景级全局可靠性和位置级局部空间可靠性两个层面联合建模可见光与红外模态的有效性，实现面向目标检测的动态自适应融合。结果在DroneVehicle和VEDAI两个公开RGB-IR数据集上，所提方法均取得了较优性能。其中，在DroneVehicle上的mAP@0.5和mAP@0.5：0.95分别达到79.7%和53.7%；在VEDAI上分别达到67.1%和30.1%，并在夜间、弱光及复杂干扰场景下表现出更好的检测精度与鲁棒性。消融实验进一步验证了模态质量属性建模、语义先验蒸馏和全局-局部模态可靠性联合建模的有效性。结论所提方法能够以较低开销将视觉语言模型的模态质量感知能力迁移至检测网络内部，在无需测试阶段额外引入大模型分支的条件下，有效建模复杂成像条件下的模态可靠性变化，提升航空遥感红外与可见光目标检测的精度与鲁棒性。

item.title: DDGA: Domain Distance Guided Active Domain Adaptation for Unpaired Super-Resolution
item.dc:creator: Zai-Zuo Tang
Yu-Bin Yang
item.description: While additional training samples enhance the performance of unpaired super-resolution (SR) networks, they inevitably escalate training costs. To address this issue, we propose a Domain Distance Guided Active Domain Adaptation (DDGA) method for unpaired SR, which intelligently selects additional training samples to maximize network performance improvement while minimizing the number of selected samples. In the sample selection process, to solve the problem of incompatibility between the additional samples and the target domain, we propose a three-fold metric that combines compatibility, complementarity, and representativeness to align the distribution of the additional samples with that of the target domain. Additionally, a domain representation method based on relative offset is proposed, transforming the task of texture detail representation into a score-based representation, effectively reducing the reliance of active domain adaptation (ADA) methods on texture details for unpaired SR tasks. Extensive experiments validate DDGA's effectiveness, demonstrating a 0.538 dB performance improvement in unpaired SR networks while introducing only 2% additional samples. Published: 2026-06-12T19:43:19+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.795 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zai-Zuo Tang; Yu-Bin Yang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3703310

Relevance: 0.795 (must_read)

While additional training samples enhance the performance of unpaired super-resolution (SR) networks, they inevitably escalate training costs. To address this issue, we propose a Domain Distance Guided Active Domain Adaptation (DDGA) method for unpaired SR, which intelligently selects additional training samples to maximize network performance improvement while minimizing the number of selected samples. In the sample selection process, to solve the problem of incompatibility between the additional samples and the target domain, we propose a three-fold metric that combines compatibility, complementarity, and representativeness to align the distribution of the additional samples with that of the target domain. Additionally, a domain representation method based on relative offset is proposed, transforming the task of texture detail representation into a score-based representation, effectively reducing the reliance of active domain adaptation (ADA) methods on texture details for unpaired SR tasks. Extensive experiments validate DDGA's effectiveness, demonstrating a 0.538 dB performance improvement in unpaired SR networks while introducing only 2% additional samples.

item.title: Cross-Modal Fusion with UVI Enhancement for Salient Object Detection in Low-Light ORSI Images
item.dc:creator: Jianlong He
HuiLan Luo
Xia Cai
Ziran Gao
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Salient object detection (SOD) in optical remote sensing images (ORSIs) remains challenging under low-light conditions, where illumination degradation severely reduces object visibility and boundary clarity. Existing ORSI-SOD methods, mainly designed for well-lit scenes, often suffer substantial performance drops in this setting. To address this issue, we propose DarkSalNet, a dual-branch framework for low-light ORSI-SOD. DarkSalNet jointly exploits the raw low-light RGB input and a UVI representation consisting of two ultraviolet-like chromatic channels (U, V ) and an intensity channel (I), generated by a perceptually guided color-space transform. Two cross-modal integration modules are further introduced: (1) a Cross-Modal Fusion (CMF) module that performs bidirectional gating for adaptive spatial feature calibration, and (2) a Semantic Cross-Aggregation (SCA) module that employs cross-attention with dual-path refinement to enhance semantic alignment. Extensive experiments on six synthetic low-light ORSI-SOD datasets and one real low-light NSI-SOD dataset demonstrate that DarkSalNet achieves state-of-the-art performance under varying illumination levels. Code and datasets are available at: https://github.com/ elaxEgan/DarkSalNet. Published: 2026-06-12T19:42:30+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.792 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jianlong He; HuiLan Luo; Xia Cai; Ziran Gao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3703225

Relevance: 0.792 (must_read)

Salient object detection (SOD) in optical remote sensing images (ORSIs) remains challenging under low-light conditions, where illumination degradation severely reduces object visibility and boundary clarity. Existing ORSI-SOD methods, mainly designed for well-lit scenes, often suffer substantial performance drops in this setting. To address this issue, we propose DarkSalNet, a dual-branch framework for low-light ORSI-SOD. DarkSalNet jointly exploits the raw low-light RGB input and a UVI representation consisting of two ultraviolet-like chromatic channels (U, V ) and an intensity channel (I), generated by a perceptually guided color-space transform. Two cross-modal integration modules are further introduced: (1) a Cross-Modal Fusion (CMF) module that performs bidirectional gating for adaptive spatial feature calibration, and (2) a Semantic Cross-Aggregation (SCA) module that employs cross-attention with dual-path refinement to enhance semantic alignment. Extensive experiments on six synthetic low-light ORSI-SOD datasets and one real low-light NSI-SOD dataset demonstrate that DarkSalNet achieves state-of-the-art performance under varying illumination levels. Code and datasets are available at: https://github.com/ elaxEgan/DarkSalNet.

item.title: Probabilistic Multiple Prototypes Contrastive Alignment for Cross-Domain Semantic Segmentation of Remote Sensing Images
item.dc:creator: Yi Sun
Bin Sun
Fuhua Zhang
Shutao Li
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) aims to transfer knowledge from a labeled source domain to an unlabeled target domain to improve model generalization, and has been widely used in remote sensing image (RSI) semantic segmentation. However, complex intra-domain category distributions and mismatched cross-domain category priors hinder accurate domain alignment in existing UDA methods. To address these challenges, we propose a probabilistic multiple prototypes contrastive alignment (PMPCA) UDA method. First, we develop a probabilistic multiple prototypes learning (PMPL) module, which constructs multiple prototypes along with probabilistic parameters to represent both the central tendency and dispersion of category distributions. This module enables fine-grained preservation of category semantics in both the source and target domains. Second, we introduce a prior-weighted contrastive (PWC) constraint, which establishes prototype-level contrastive learning between the source and target domains. It pulls prototypes toward their corresponding categories while pushing them away from other categories, thereby promoting structural alignment across domains. Furthermore, we estimate category frequencies in both domains and use them as prior weights for adaptive calibration, mitigating bias induced by category prior and refining alignment. Finally, we design an uncertainty-guided curriculum adaptation (UCA) strategy, which quantifies the difficulty of target domain based on prediction uncertainty and enables progressive UDA from easy to hard cases. This strategy reduces the interference of noisy pseudo-labels. Extensive experiments on six UDA settings demonstrate that PMPCA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods and generalizes well across spatial regions and spectral bands. Published: 2026-06-12T19:42:30+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.791 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yi Sun; Bin Sun; Fuhua Zhang; Shutao Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3703069

Relevance: 0.791 (must_read)

Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) aims to transfer knowledge from a labeled source domain to an unlabeled target domain to improve model generalization, and has been widely used in remote sensing image (RSI) semantic segmentation. However, complex intra-domain category distributions and mismatched cross-domain category priors hinder accurate domain alignment in existing UDA methods. To address these challenges, we propose a probabilistic multiple prototypes contrastive alignment (PMPCA) UDA method. First, we develop a probabilistic multiple prototypes learning (PMPL) module, which constructs multiple prototypes along with probabilistic parameters to represent both the central tendency and dispersion of category distributions. This module enables fine-grained preservation of category semantics in both the source and target domains. Second, we introduce a prior-weighted contrastive (PWC) constraint, which establishes prototype-level contrastive learning between the source and target domains. It pulls prototypes toward their corresponding categories while pushing them away from other categories, thereby promoting structural alignment across domains. Furthermore, we estimate category frequencies in both domains and use them as prior weights for adaptive calibration, mitigating bias induced by category prior and refining alignment. Finally, we design an uncertainty-guided curriculum adaptation (UCA) strategy, which quantifies the difficulty of target domain based on prediction uncertainty and enables progressive UDA from easy to hard cases. This strategy reduces the interference of noisy pseudo-labels. Extensive experiments on six UDA settings demonstrate that PMPCA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods and generalizes well across spatial regions and spectral bands.

item.title: Negation-Aware Test-Time Adaptation for Vision-Language Models
item.dc:creator: Haochen Han
Alex Jinpeng Wang
Fangming Liu
Jun Zhu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: In this paper, we study a practical but less-touched problem in Vision-Language Models (VLMs), i.e., negation understanding. Specifically, many real-world applications require models to explicitly identify what is false or non-existent, e.g., radiologists may search for images that exclude specific conditions. Despite the impressive transferability of VLMs through large-scale training, they suffer from a critical limitation that fails to handle negation. To address this challenge, existing methods attribute its root cause to the scarcity of negation training data and propose to fine-tune VLMs on massive data containing explicit negation. Undoubtedly, such data-centric solutions demand substantial data and computational resources, limiting their sustainable widespread adoption. To tackle negation in a low-carbon manner, we empirically observe that the key obstacle lies in the dual-concept shifts between the affirmation and negation distributions. Therefore, we propose a Negation-Aware Test-Time Adaptation (NEAT) method to efficiently adjust distribution-related parameters during inference. In brief, NEAT can reduce distribution shift in consistent semantics while eliminating false distributional consistency in unrelated semantics. Extensive experiments on the various negation understanding tasks verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. Remarkably, with less than 0.01% of trainable parameters, NEAT achieves comparable or superior performance to state-of-the-art post-training approaches. Our code is available at https://github.com/hhc1997/NEAT. Published: 2026-06-12T19:42:29+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.791 (must_read)
